literal_meaning: these, bhikkhus, are the five heaps
preferred_translation: these, bhikkhus, are the five heaps
alternative_translations: these, bhikkhus, are the five aggregates
definition: Canonical heading for the plain five-heaps set.
notes: Use this phrase record when the text is introducing the fivefold set as a coordinated doctrinal family. The formula inherits the house rule for khandha and keeps five heaps as the default. The aggregate alternate is allowed for explanatory prose, not as the repository default in direct translation.

context_rules.context: In direct doctrinal translation of the standard heading
context_rules.rendering: these, bhikkhus, are the five heaps

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose using familiar Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.rendering: these, bhikkhus, are the five aggregates
context_rules.notes: Use this in explanatory prose only for orientation.

example_phrases.translation: these, bhikkhus, are the five heaps
example_phrases.notes: Shows the canonical heading for the plain set.
